page: Amazon Web Services is4gen.8xlarge is a storage-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It features 32 dedicated physical cores powered by the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 192 GB of system memory, establishing a 1:6 vCPU-to-memory ratio. A key hardware highlight is the inclusion of 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates balanced mid-range performance across most compute and memory workloads. The instance achieved a multi-core stress-ng score of 48,576.33 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11,813.0, both falling within the average tier. Memory bandwidth is robust, with cached small-block reads reaching 1,894.37 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 170.35 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff; the Geekbench single-core score of 971.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 20.34 GB/sec are classified in the weak tier. Similarly, LLM text generation with a small 135M model is poor at 24.55 tokens/sec, though medium 7B model inference remains average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the is4gen.8xlarge offers high local storage density and high network throughput relative to its ARM-based CPU profile. The dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading ensure predictable compute performance for storage-heavy workloads. This instance is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput static web serving, and data-intensive applications requiring massive local NVMe capacity, while being less optimal for single-threaded compute tasks or intensive LLM generation.
description: The Amazon Web Services is4gen.8xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance powered by a 32-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 192 GB of RAM and an extensive 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48,576.33 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11,813.0. However, single-core performance represents a tradeoff, with weak single-threaded compression and low single-core Geekbench scores. This instance provides high storage density and predictable dedicated compute resources, making it highly efficient for storage-intensive applications. It is best suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput static web serving, and data-intensive workloads requiring massive local NVMe capacity.
og_description: Amazon Web Services is4gen.8xlarge is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, 30 TB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services is4gen.8xlarge is a storage-optimized ARM64 instance with 32 Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 30 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized ARM64 platform delivering thirty terabytes of local NVMe SSD capacity and fifty gigabits per second network bandwidth.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 cores at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 192 GB of RAM and 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Demonstrates average multi-core performance but weaker single-core execution
Optimized for large-scale databases and high-throughput storage workloads
